lines.text: Extending on the curb in the pharmaceutical blow.
CVS fluorescent, got that receipt for show.
Forty dollar copay, but I'm feeling like a king.
Crinkle of the bag is my everything.
Since four minutes, I got nowhere to be.
Just me in the parking lot.
in my new pharmacy little white bag little label with my name before the waymo pulls up nothing's
ever the same no no small talk no oxcore negotiation no how's your day obligatory
conversation just sensors just light up just the open road me my meds in a car that's in flow state
mode waiting for the waymo feeling kind of lame-o standing in the pickup zone like a little flamingo
but it's pulling up now white and clean and quiet four wheels of freedom zero human right
take me home me and my prescriptions riding alone
waymo waymo no one at the wheel this is the most autonomous i will
side in the backseat door side shut like a sigh sanitize the smell and the nuisance of vibe reading
the side effects like a poet you denied a boy grapefruit noted yeah i'll be all right the city
bends around us every light turns green most cinematic moment that i've ever seen no one to
impress no tip percentage map just staring at the window while my healing fat no radio unless i pick
the station no judgment on my posture for my destination camera sees me sure but it don't care
